content: Standard pattern for Node.js CLI integration tests: use execFileSync/spawnSync from node:child_process to invoke the CLI binary as a subprocess; capture stdout/stderr; assert on exit code and output content. Existing agentmap-php tests (helpers.mjs run()) already follow this pattern with makeRepo/gitInit/cleanup helpers. Integration tests against real fixtures differ from unit tests: they require the fixture to exist on disk (no temp repo creation), pass real flags (--packages, --types, --legacy) against the pre-cloned laravel/framework directory, and assert on known outputs (e.g. 'laravel/framework' in stdout for --packages). Key risk: fixture path may not exist in CI if eval/bench fixtures are not committed or pre-cloned — must either commit a minimal fixture subset or skip test when fixture absent. Existing eval.mjs clones laravel/framework to tmp/eval/ at runtime; CI must clone or the integration test must guard with existsSync.
